msgs.text:
I treated the prior turns as proposals rather than proof. The prompt‚Äôs required format is the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only serious unrefuted candidate remains the dynamic tame-inertia word; the earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is immaterial in this occurrence because ŌĄ has prime-to-2 order in every finite tame quotient.The two algebraic conventions that should now be frozen are these.First, in the sign-cover calculation, if t is the deck involution of V=kerőļ, and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character, then forNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.It is best not to write this as an informal fraction until the equation is stated:(1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít,because ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) on t and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th odd tame character. ThereforeDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

This is the direction that makes the argument work: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient of x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.Second, in the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

When f=2a m with m odd, s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The valid elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.

Equivalently, Y=Ps (T)X+Y, up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one gets, for ő∂f=1, ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1‚Č°1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. This supports the prior cup-product nonsingularity argument, but only with this convention.My new point concerns Turn 53‚Äôs affine-versus-linear correction. Turn 53 correctly observed thatH1(P,L2H)classifies differences of marked Frobenius lifts, not the raw first IA term of one lift. I found a useful canonical affine origin for the b-component, which makes that correction operational.

Let f=2a m, let H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü© be the odd Hall subgroup of a finite tame quotient őĒe,f, and let P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a. In the reduced first graded piece,L1‚ČÉF2[őĒe,f]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S,the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial relation isq=g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].

For a marked lift őĪ of the P-generator satisfying őĪ2a=Inn(S), write the first IA term on b asmb=őĪ(b)b‚ąí1‚ąąL2H.

Then mb satisfies the affine norm equationNP(mb)=[S,b].

The relation q=0 gives [S,b]=[b,S]=g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎXg[2].

Now defineőėb=h‚ąąH‚ąĎXh[2].ThenNP(őėb)=p‚ąąP‚ąĎh‚ąąH‚ąĎXhp[2]=g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎXg[2]=[S,b].

So őėb is a canonical particular solution of the affine norm equation, once the H-coset origin and Frobenius lift convention are fixed. Therefore the genuine homogeneous comparison class is[mb‚ąíőėb]‚ąąkerNP/(ŌÉňČ‚ąí1)L2H=H1(P,L2H).This is not just bookkeeping. It means any prior statement of the form ‚Äúthe candidate‚Äôs raw b-component is Zj ‚ÄĚ is incomplete unless őėb has already been subtracted or a different affine origin has been declared. The involution-indexed Zj ‚Äôs live in the homogeneous difference space. A single marked lift must also carry the affine square-sum component forced by őĪ2a=Inn(S).

For (e,f)=(3,2), this says the previously isolated Z0,Z1,Z2 should be interpreted as residual homogeneous classes after subtracting the affine origin őėb, or else the computation must be rechecked against the marked power condition. For (e,f)=(5,4), the sharp next target is now:mb‚ąíőėb projected to the two universal square directions and the five involution-indexed directions. Only that residual class is comparable with an arithmetic-minus-candidate class. Comparing a raw mb vector to zero would be a category error.So the live candidate remains unrefuted, but the comparison problem is now more precisely affine: choose a concrete tame field, choose the same Frobenius-lift gauge, subtract the canonical affine particular solution őėb, and compare the resulting homogeneous class in H1(P,L2H).

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. The stable formulas areDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.New this turn: for the first marked IA equation, the b-component has a canonical affine particular solutionőėb=h‚ąąH‚ąĎXh[2],because NP(őėb)=[S,b]. Thus H1(P,L2H) should be used for mb‚ąíőėb, not for the raw mb of a single lift.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action agrees with arithmetic over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: In the (e,f)=(5,4) quotient, compute the residual candidate class mb‚ąíőėb in the two square directions and five involution-indexed Zj -directions, in a fixed Frobenius gauge. Then compare that homogeneous residual with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the corresponding tame degree-20 extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
